name: uTuring Machine
description: Random looping sequencer built on a 16-bit shift register. A length control locks or mutates a clocked CV loop, with expander headers for pulse and voltage variations.

controls.name: Length
controls.description: Selects the loop length in steps. Short lengths produce tight phrases; longer lengths reveal more of the register's internal pattern.
controls.detail: step length selector · doubles the effective loop when the loop control is at the locked extreme

controls.name: Loop / Probability
controls.description: Sets how likely the register is to keep its current state versus slipping into a new mutation. Noon is the most changeable region.
controls.detail: locked at the extremes · mutable through the middle

controls.name: Write
controls.description: Forces the next incoming bit high or low while held. Used to sculpt a looping phrase without fully breaking the pattern.

inputs.name: CLOCK IN
inputs.description: Advances the shift register on each rising edge.

inputs.name: KNOB CV
inputs.description: CV over the loop/probability control for voltage-driven mutation.

outputs.name: CV OUT
outputs.description: Main stepped voltage output from the current register state.

outputs.name: PULSE OUT
outputs.description: Gate-style pulse output derived from the register state and shared clock.

behaviors.name: Shift-register sequencing
behaviors.description: Each pulse shifts the internal 16-bit register and produces a new stepped voltage. The pattern can stay fully locked or drift into evolving mutation depending on the loop control.

behaviors.name: Loop locking
behaviors.description: At the locked extremes the current phrase repeats. Small offsets away from lock introduce gradual changes instead of hard resets.

behaviors.name: Write editing
behaviors.description: Holding Write forces incoming bits high or low so a performer can paint a phrase into the loop without rebuilding the sequence from scratch.

behaviors.name: Expander bus
behaviors.description: The shared bus can drive expander panels such as Pulse and Volts, turning the core sequence into extra gate and CV streams without changing the main panel.

behaviors.name: Audio-rate clocking
behaviors.description: Audio-rate clocking pushes the register into metallic, alias-heavy stepping. The output behaves more like digital percussion than a conventional melody generator.

patchIdeas.name: Locked melody
patchIdeas.patch: Clock from a master sequencer · Length at 8 · Loop fully locked · quantize CV OUT to a minor scale · use PULSE OUT for envelope retriggers · a repeatable melody that still slips when the loop knob is nudged.
patchIdeas.diagram:
uTuring Machine:
* Length: 8
* Loop: locked

Clock:
Quantizer:
VCO:
Envelope:
VCA:

- Clock (Out) c> uTuring Machine (CLOCK IN)
- uTuring Machine (CV OUT) p> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- uTuring Machine (PULSE OUT) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Probabilistic bass
patchIdeas.patch: Slow LFO into KNOB CV · Loop set near noon · Length 16 · probability wanders between locked and mutable states · quantized CV OUT drives a bass VCO while PULSE OUT clocks hats.
patchIdeas.diagram:
uTuring Machine:
* Length: 16

LFO:
Quantizer:
Bass VCO:
Hat:

- LFO (Out) >> uTuring Machine (KNOB CV)
- Clock (Out) c> uTuring Machine (CLOCK IN)
- uTuring Machine (CV OUT) p> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> Bass VCO (V/Oct)
- uTuring Machine (PULSE OUT) t> Hat (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Write-switch loop sculpting
patchIdeas.patch: Lock the loop, then tap Write on selected clocks to force highs or lows into the register · the pattern becomes a performance instrument rather than a static preset · CV OUT stays melodic while PULSE OUT gets re-shaped.
patchIdeas.diagram:
uTuring Machine:
* Length: 12
* Loop: locked

Clock:
VCO:
Envelope:
VCA:

- Clock (Out) c> uTuring Machine (CLOCK IN)
- uTuring Machine (CV OUT) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- uTuring Machine (PULSE OUT) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Pulse expander groove
patchIdeas.patch: Add the Pulse expander · route several derived pulse outputs to kick, snare and percussion voices · keep the core loop locked for repeatability while the pulse subset creates polyrhythms.
patchIdeas.diagram:
uTuring Machine:
* Length: 16
* Loop: locked

Pulse:

Kick:
Snare:
Perc:

- Clock (Out) c> uTuring Machine (CLOCK IN)
- uTuring Machine (PULSE OUT) t> Pulse (In)
- Pulse (Out 1) t> Kick (Trig)
- Pulse (Out 2) t> Snare (Trig)
- Pulse (Out 4) t> Perc (Trig)
